references.name: Lockheed Martin
references.takeaway: Organize content into domain compartments as top-level navigation categories, and present each content card with mission-focused language and technical system imagery that conveys operational capability.

applied.description: Ten numbered operational sections (01-10) function as classified compartments of the interface, each with its own domain (COMMAND CENTER, OPERATIONS MANIFEST, MISSION BOARD, etc.)

applied.element: Project cards on Operations Manifest view
applied.description: Each operation card features a military codename (IRON VEIL, GHOST MERIDIAN), mission-focused descriptive copy emphasizing operational readiness, and classification level badges
Full-bleed dark hero with bold Bebas Neue headline ('AEGIS COMMAND CENTER') projecting institutional authority, paired with a typed monospace subtitle conveying threat-level status

references.name: General Dynamics
references.takeaway: Use a steel-grey and navy bi-tonal palette for all surfaces, and display content metrics as labeled specification data points in a structured grid that reads like a tactical systems readout.

applied.description: Olive-dark (#2a2e20) and olive-surface (#353828) bi-tonal palette with tan text creating a hardened-surface material language, adapted from steel-grey/navy to military olive/concrete
Specification-panel content blocks with labeled data points (Range, Weight, Power Req, Crew Size, Status) displayed in a grid format reading as a tactical systems readout with NSN codes
Four metric viewport windows displaying labeled data (ACTIVE OPERATIONS: 17, DEPLOYED ASSETS: 142) in structured bunker-slit panels resembling specification readouts

references.name: Palantir Technologies
references.takeaway: Use thin white line-art network diagrams and constellation-style node graphs as decorative hero elements on pure black backgrounds, and space content at one element per viewport height to create the sparse, deliberate pacing of a classified intelligence briefing.

applied.description: SVG tactical map with thin line-art connections between strategic position nodes (FOB GRANITE, RADAR ECHO-1, THAAD ALPHA) on a dark grid background, creating a network-graph visual language
Spacious editorial sections with substantial vertical spacing (64-128px between sections) creating the sparse, deliberate pacing of a classified intelligence briefing
Each intelligence brief presented as a single focused element with classification header, body text, and metadata — sparse typography with one element per section creating briefing-document pacing
